title: exostema caribaeum
intro: Exostema caribaeum, also known as Jamaica quinine or Wild Quinine, is a flowering plant native to the Caribbean and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Exostema caribaeum is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves are also used to make a poultice for topical application.
